hero.tagline: Junior Fullstack Developer with hands-on experience building real products from frontend to backend.
hero.focus: I build applications with React, Next.js, Node.js, and databases, with attention to user experience, business logic, and production delivery.

barber.description:
💈 Management system for barbershops focused on daily operations, scheduling, and customer relationships.

🧩 It addresses common problems such as booking conflicts, scattered customer history, and fragmented communication.

⚙️ Highlights:
• real-time scheduling
• customer records and service history
• conversation management with Chatwoot
• AI support to interpret messages and speed up service flow

🧰 Stack: Next.js, TypeScript, PostgreSQL, and Tailwind.
🚀 Strong point: structure designed for multiple locations, with clear separation between interface, business logic, and data.

habit.description:
🧠 Habit tracking application with an AI layer designed to provide more contextual and personalized guidance.

🧩 The goal was to move beyond a simple checklist and build an experience that helps users understand behavior patterns.

⚙️ Highlights:
• continuous habit tracking
• persistent per-user history
• real-time AI responses
• accumulated context for more relevant recommendations

🧰 Stack: React, MongoDB, custom backend, and the Groq API.
🚀 Strong point: integration between tracking, context, and prompts to create a less generic experience.

market.description:
📊 Real-time financial dashboard for tracking assets, portfolio, and market movement in a single interface.

🧩 The main challenge was handling frequent updates without hurting usability.

⚙️ Highlights:
• real-time data through WebSocket
• P&L visualization and asset tracking
• state architecture organized with multiple contexts
• report export and multi-currency support

🧰 Stack: React 18, Chart.js, WebSocket, and Vite.
🚀 Strong point: balance between responsive UI and continuous data flow.

km.description:
🚚 Financial application for independent truck drivers to calculate freight profitability and track operation results.

🧩 The core problem was replacing spreadsheets and manual calculations with a simple decision-making tool.

⚙️ Highlights:
• net profit calculation per freight
• quick margin visualization
• financial organization by period
• PDF and CSV reports

🧰 Stack: Next.js, TypeScript, Tailwind, Supabase, and jsPDF.
🚀 Strong point: objective interface for operational usage, with integrated authentication and persistence.

watchDog.description:
👀 Full stack uptime monitoring platform for registering endpoints, running scheduled checks, and sending automated alerts.

🧩 The project was built to exercise job-oriented backend flows, Redis state handling, and service integration.

⚙️ Highlights:
• React dashboard for monitor management
• Fastify API with TypeScript
• workers protected against duplicate execution
• email alerts through Brevo and n8n
• local Docker setup and validated Railway deployment

🧰 Stack: React, Node.js, Fastify, Redis, n8n, and Docker.
🚀 Strong point: complete monitoring flow with automation and clear separation of responsibilities.

startup.title: Autonomus
startup.startupSubtitle: Mobile marketplace startup where I contributed to product development and technical foundations, connecting clients and service providers.
startup.problemDesc: Hiring independent services is still fragmented, unsafe, and inefficient. Clients struggle to find trusted professionals, while workers have low visibility and limited access to opportunities.
startup.solution1: direct connection between clients and service providers
startup.solution3: digital layer to make the process safer
startup.solution4: simple experience for both sides of the marketplace
startup.diff1: separate apps for clients and service providers
startup.diff2: backend centralizing business rules and API logic
startup.diff3: admin dashboard for operations and support
startup.diff4: Docker-based local setup for consistent development
startup.diff5: structure designed to evolve without excessive coupling
startup.impact3: improves operational clarity for both sides of the marketplace
startup.impact4: creates a foundation for product validation and future evolution
startup.educationImpactDesc: I worked as a robotics instructor at UTFPR, teaching programming logic and computational thinking to children from public schools. I planned and led hands-on activities with LEGO and Scratch focused on problem solving.
startup.educationImpact1: hands-on LEGO and Scratch classes for children
startup.educationImpact2: development of logical reasoning and computational thinking
startup.educationImpact3: methods focused on creativity and problem solving
startup.cacompSubtitle: Member of UTFPR's Computer Science Academic Center
startup.cacompDesc: I served as a member of UTFPR's Computer Science Academic Center (CACOMP), helping organize events, talks, and initiatives focused on the academic and professional development of Computer Science students.
startup.cacomp1: organization of events and talks for the academic community
startup.cacomp2: promotion of initiatives for students' professional development
startup.cacomp3: encouragement of hands-on learning and student networking
startup.cacomp4: strengthening student engagement in Computer Science
startup.recognitionDesc: Autonomus was featured by RPC/Globo on the Rocket Startup program.
startup.recognitionCta: See more in the Media tab

startup.media4: digitalization of a still understructured market
startup.mediaCardDesc: The media appearance brought public validation and reinforced the project's potential.
